summary:
Place n queens on an n x n chessboard so that no two queens attack each other, meaning no two share a row, column, or diagonal.

Return every distinct valid arrangement. Each solution is described as a list of n strings, one per row, where 'Q' marks a queen and '.' marks an empty square.

examples.explanation: There are exactly two ways to position four non-attacking queens on a 4x4 board.
A single queen on a 1x1 board trivially attacks nothing.

constraints: Exactly n queens must be placed, one per row and one per column
No two queens may lie on the same row, column, or diagonal
Each board uses 'Q' for a queen and '.' for an empty cell
For n = 2 and n = 3 no valid arrangement exists, so the result is empty
Solutions may be returned in any order
